2m.name: 2 Metres
2m.notes: Most popular VHF band in the UK. Repeaters use 600kHz split. SSB calling on 144.300 MHz.

70cm.name: 70 Centimetres
70cm.notes: Popular UHF band. Repeaters use 1.6MHz split. Foundation limit applies.

6m.name: 6 Metres
6m.notes: Known as the magic band due to sporadic E propagation.

4m.name: 4 Metres
4m.notes: UK only allocation. Not available in most other countries.

40m.name: 40 Metres
40m.notes: Excellent for medium distance HF comms, especially at night.

20m.name: 20 Metres
20m.notes: Most popular HF DX band. Good worldwide propagation during the day.

80m.name: 80 Metres
80m.notes: Good for UK and European contacts. Heavy QRM in evenings.

160m.name: 160 Metres
160m.notes: The top band. Difficult propagation, best at night in winter.

10m.name: 10 Metres
10m.notes: Excellent DX when solar cycle is high. Foundation licence permitted.

15m.name: 15 Metres
15m.notes: Good DX band, best during daylight hours.

30m.name: 30 Metres
30m.notes: WARC band — no contests permitted. Narrow allocation, CW and digital only.

23cm.name: 23 Centimetres
23cm.notes: Microwave band. Good for local contacts and satellite work.
